payload.tool_response: Here's the full picture of the explored codebase: --- ## 1. `src/views/DashboardView.vue` Minimal placeholder — no real content yet: ```vue ``` --- ## 2. `src/stores/auth.ts` Full Pinia store with `token`, `user`, `isAuthenticated`, `setToken`, `setUser`, `handleCallback`, `login`, `logout`. Persists token to `localStorage` and syncs `OpenAPI.TOKEN`. --- ## 3. `src/stores/auth.spec.ts` Full test suite covering `handleCallback`, `login`, and `logout` — 8 test cases using `vi.mock`, Pinia `createPinia`, and `makeJwt` helper. --- ## 4. `src/lib/api/` — directory contents ### `index.ts` Re-exports: `ApiError`, `CancelablePromise`, `CancelError`, `OpenAPI`, all model types, and `DefaultService`. ### `core/OpenAPI.ts` `OpenAPIConfig` type + singleton `OpenAPI` object with `BASE: ''`, `VERSION: '0.1.0'`, `WITH_CREDENTIALS: false`, `CREDENTIALS: 'include'`, token/auth fields `undefined`. ### `models/AuthTokenBody.ts` — `{ $schema?, token: string }` ### `models/ErrorDetail.ts` — `{ location?, message?, value? }` ### `models/ErrorModel.ts` — `{ $schema?, detail?, errors?, instance?, status?, title?, type? }` ### `models/HelloResponseBody.ts` — `{ $schema?, message: string }` ### `models/RegisterRequestBody.ts` — `{ $schema?, pre_registration_token: string, user_name: string }` ### `services/DefaultService.ts` Three methods: - `logout()` — `POST /auth/logout` - `registerUser({ requestBody })` — `POST /auth/register` - `hello()` — `GET /hello` **No URL-related methods exist in the generated client yet.** --- ## 5. `src/router/index.ts` Three routes: `/` (HomeView), `/dashboard` (DashboardView, `requiresAuth: true`), `/auth/callback` (AuthCallbackView). Navigation guard: unauthenticated → home, authenticated on home → dashboard. --- ## 6. `src/lib/config.ts` Config validation/logging utility. `REQUIRED_VARS = ['LINKSHORTENER_ENV', 'APP_BASE_URL']`, `SECRET_VARS` set, `maskedValue()`, `validateConfig()`, `logConfig()`. --- ## 7. OpenAPI `/user/urls` endpoint + URL schemas **`GET /user/urls`** (`operationId: list-user-urls`): - Query params: `page` (int64, min 1, default 1), `page_size` (int64, max 100) - 200: `ListUrlsResponseBody` - default: `ErrorModel` **`ListUrlsResponseBody` schema:** ```json { "items": UrlItem[] | null, "total": int64, "page": int64, "page_size": int64 } ``` Required: `items`, `total`, `page`, `page_size` **`UrlItem` schema:** ```json { "id": int64, "shortcode": string, "long_url": string, "created_at": date-time, "updated_at": date-time, "expires_at"?: date-time } ``` Required: `id`, `shortcode`, `long_url`, `created_at`, `updated_at` --- ## URL store check **No URLs store exists.** [frontend/src/stores/](frontend/src/stores/) only contains [auth.ts](frontend/src/stores/auth.ts) and [auth.spec.ts](frontend/src/stores/auth.spec.ts). The generated API client ([DefaultService.ts](frontend/src/lib/api/services/DefaultService.ts)) also has **no URL-related methods** — it needs to be regenerated after the backend's `/user/urls` endpoint was added, and a `urls` Pinia store needs to be created from scratch.

package routes_test

import (
	"net/http"
	"net/http/httptest"
	"testing"

	"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
	"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"

	"github.com/AlexL70/linkshortener/backend/web/routes"
)

// newCORSRouter builds a minimal Gin router with CORSMiddleware and a stub GET
// handler at "/test", so individual tests can inspect response headers.
func newCORSRouter(t *testing.T) *gin.Engine {
	t.Helper()
	gin.SetMode(gin.TestMode)
	r := gin.New()
	r.Use(routes.CORSMiddleware())
	r.GET("/test", func(c *gin.Context) {
		c.Status(http.StatusOK)
	})
	return r
}

func TestCORSMiddleware_DevMode_AllowsAnyOrigin(t *testing.T) {
	t.Setenv("LINKSHORTENER_ENV", "dev")
	t.Setenv("APP_BASE_URL", "http://localhost:8080")

	router := newCORSRouter(t)

	req := httptest.NewRequest(http.MethodGet, "/test", nil)
	req.Header.Set("Origin", "http://localhost:5173")
	w := httptest.NewRecorder()
	router.ServeHTTP(w, req)

	assert.Equal(t, http.StatusOK, w.Code)
